scene.description: Engine 181 has a 2½″ line stretched to a gated wye. Two 1¾″ handlines are operating from the wye.

problems.title: 2½″ Supply to Wye — Two Handlines
problems.question: Engine 181 has a 2½″ line stretched to a gated wye. Two 1¾″ handlines are operating from the wye. Main line: 200′ of 2½″ hose from engine to wye. Branch A: 200′ of 1¾″ hose, 165 gpm fog nozzle @ 50 psi. Branch B: 250′ of 1¾″ hose, 150 gpm fog nozzle @ 100 psi. Wye appliance loss: 10 psi. No elevation change What pump pressure should Engine 181 set?
problems.instructorExplanation: Total flow through the 2½″ main is 315 gpm. Main FL = 2 × (315/100)² × 2 = 40 psi. Branch A needs 84 FL + 50 NP = 134 psi. Branch B needs 87 FL + 100 NP = 187 psi. Use the higher branch: 187 + 40 main FL + 10 wye loss = 237 psi.
problems.explainMistake: Common errors: adding both branch pressures together, forgetting wye loss, or calculating the main line at only one branch flow instead of total flow.

problems.title: 2½″ Supply to Wye — Two Handlines
problems.question: Engine 181 is feeding a gated wye. Both 1¾″ branch lines are now flowing 185 gpm at 50 psi. Branch A is 200′, Branch B is 250′, the 2½″ main is 200′, and wye loss is 10 psi. What pump pressure should you set?
formulaBreakdown: Branch A FL = 15.5 × (185 ÷ 100)^2 × 2 = 106 psi; Branch A total = 156 psi
Branch B FL = 15.5 × (185 ÷ 100)^2 × 2.5 = 133 psi; Branch B total = 183 psi
Use the higher branch: 183 + 55 main FL + 10 wye loss = 248 psi
problems.instructorExplanation: For a wye, do not add both branch pressures. Use the higher branch requirement, then add the main-line friction loss and appliance loss.
problems.explainMistake: Common errors: adding both branch pressures together, forgetting wye loss, or calculating the main line at only one branch flow instead of total flow.

problems.title: 2½″ Supply to Wye — Two Handlines
problems.question: Engine 181 has a 2½″ line stretched to a gated wye. Two 1¾″ handlines are operating from the wye. Original wye setup. Main line: 200′ of 2½″ hose from engine to wye. Branch A: 200′ of 1¾″ hose, 165 gpm fog nozzle @ 50 psi. Branch B: 250′ of 1¾″ hose, 150 gpm fog nozzle @ 100 psi. Wye appliance loss: 10 psi. Elevation: +20′ above pump, approximately +10 psi What pump pressure should Engine 181 set?
formulaBreakdown: Elevation pressure = approximately +10 psi for 20′ above the pump
problems.instructorExplanation: This run reuses the same wye picture but adds elevation pressure to the final pump pressure.
problems.explainMistake: Common errors: adding both branch pressures together, forgetting wye loss, or calculating the main line at only one branch flow instead of total flow.
